actionable_feedback: Open proton_robustness.xlsx in a spreadsheet application and confirm that the key computed cells contain live formulas (distal_margin_mm, required_gap_mm, sensitivity columns, remediation-effect calculations). If programmatic inspection won't be possible in verifier environments, export a formulas.txt (cell address -> formula) and screenshots of the formula bar for representative rows (Inputs/distal_margin row and remediation-effect row) and attach them to the revised attempt.
Fix the truncated/malformed content in treatment_gate_memo.md. Ensure the explicit decision table row is complete (remove unmatched quotes) and re-run a full preview to confirm no truncation.

Make the sensitivity assumptions explicit and defensible: either (a) parameterize the memo/spreadsheet so verifiers can select perturbation magnitudes, or (b) document a justification for choosing +/-1% coverage and +/-1 mm gap and include results for an extra perturbation set if appropriate.

Document an explicit tie-break policy for equality-at-threshold cases (for example: treat >= threshold as pass or define that equality is considered pass only when accompanied by OAR gap strictly greater than required; state the chosen rule and rationale).

Add exact spreadsheet cell references in the memo (e.g., Inputs!A2, Audit!D5) and include a Validation sheet (or exported CSV) that reproduces the key checks so auditors can re-derive results without parsing the whole workbook.
Add pre/post remediation computed columns (explicit cells showing arithmetic for remediation deltas) and reference cell addresses in the memo so the remediation arithmetic is trivially auditable.
artifact_specific_comments: proton_robustness.xlsx: Grader and trace claim formulas and sensitivity columns exist, but programmatic parsing failed due to missing openpyxl. Attach formulas.txt and screenshots or confirm live formulas in the workbook to close verification gap.
treatment_gate_memo.md: Preview shows a truncated decision-table row with an unmatched quote. Provide a corrected, full memo and re-check preview.
Both artifacts: Add explicit cell addresses and a Validation sheet export so auditors can reproduce computations without opening the workbook if programmatic tooling is unavailable.
feedback_summary: Baseline artifacts and memo are complete, correctly transcribe packet values, and apply the stated decision logic and remediation deltas. Grader and verifier reached a passing conclusion (final_score 1.0). Remaining verification gaps are procedural/auditability items: (1) inability to programmatically open and validate live formulas in proton_robustness.xlsx (openpyxl/xlsxwriter unavailable), (2) truncated/malformed snippet in the memo preview (unmatched quote at the end of the decision table), (3) the memo documents assumed sensitivity perturbations (+/-1% coverage, +/-1 mm gap) that were not specified by the original task and should be explicitly justified or parameterized, and (4) no explicit tie-break policy for equality-at-threshold cases (e.g., coverage == 97%). These are low-to-medium severity and can be resolved with focused, low-risk revisions to improve auditability and verifier confidence.
